name: Bluestone Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Bluestone. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Bluestone cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength optimized for Bluestone mineral absorption

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse width for controlled ablation of contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete cleaning

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform coverage

power.description: Typical range for industrial cleaning of natural stones like Bluestone using 1064 nm fiber lasers; starting at 100W balances efficiency and safety to avoid thermal damage to porous substrate. Max limited by Bluestone's low thermal conductivity (~1-2 W/m·K) to prevent cracking. Source: Manufacturer specs from IPG Photonics YLR series and academic review in 'Laser Cleaning of Stone Surfaces' (J. Cult. Herit., 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contamination removal on stone; slower (500-1000 mm/s) for heavy rust/graffiti, faster (2000-3000 mm/s) for light dust. Recommended 1500 mm/s for optimal balance on Bluestone to ensure uniform cleaning without overheating. Source: Industry standards from Adapt Laser Systems manuals and case studies in Opt. Lasers Eng. (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime (10-200 ns) preferred for cost-effective cleaning of stones; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond reduces heat-affected zone (HAZ) but increases cost. 50 ns recommended for Bluestone to minimize thermal diffusion given its melting point ~1200-1400°C. Source: Laser physics research in Appl. Phys. A (2019) on stone ablation thresholds.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning to control energy delivery; higher rates (>50 kHz) speed up process but risk heat accumulation on low-conductivity Bluestone. 50 kHz balances speed and safety. Trade-off: high freq increases throughput but requires monitoring for substrate heating. Source: Academic literature in J. Appl. Phys. (2021) on pulse repetition in heritage cleaning.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe removal of contaminants like soot or oxides; Bluestone damage threshold ~4-5 J/cm² due to moderate reflectivity (~20-30% at 1064 nm). Recommended 1.5 J/cm² avoids ablation of substrate while effective for industrial prep. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Surf. Coat. Technol. (2017) for sandstones.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for cleaning; smaller (100-200 μm) for fine details on textured Bluestone, larger (300-500 μm) for broad areas. 200 μm recommended for uniform coverage in surface preparation. Source: Equipment specifications from CleanLaser CL series and process optimization in Lasers Manuf. Mater. Process. (2022).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy layers to build fluence gradually and prevent damage. 2 passes recommended for Bluestone to ensure complete removal without excessive heat exposure. Source: Industry practice in case studies from Laser Cleaning Europe consortium and Opt. Express (2019).

overlap_ratio.description: Overlap ensures uniform fluence distribution; 30-50% for fast large-area cleaning, 60-80% for detailed work on irregular Bluestone surfaces. 60% recommended for even coverage in contamination removal. Source: Process optimization studies in J. Laser Appl. (2020) for stone substrates.
